name: Elven Chess
author: H. G. Muller (2014) / AGP port
description: H. G. Muller's 10x10 hybrid of orthodox Chess and Chu Shogi (2014). The full FIDE army is joined by four crowned pieces: the Goblin (rook + king step), Elf (bishop + king step), Dwarf (a king-stepping commoner) and the mighty Warlock — the Chu-Shogi Lion, which may take two king-steps in one turn: capture twice, capture and run, capture and return (igui), or step out-and-back to pass. Two signature rules make Warlocks nearly impossible to trade: a Warlock may capture the other Warlock only onto a king-safe square, and after a non-Warlock captures a Warlock the survivor is 'iron' (uncapturable) for one turn. Pawns promote on entering the last three ranks (to Q/R/B/N only); the king castles three squares. Checkmate wins.
